nodes.name: White-shouldered black tit
nodes.summary: The white-shouldered black tit (Melaniparus guineensis), also known as the pale-eyed black tit, is a passerine bird in the tit family. It breeds in a belt across Africa from Senegal in the west to Kenya and Ethiopia in the east. It is sometimes considered conspecific with the more southerly white-winged black tit Melaniparus leucomelas and, like that species, it is mainly black with a white wing patch, but differs in that it has a pale eye. It is a resident in coniferous woodlands throughout its range, and nests in tree crevice. 4-6 reddish-brown blotched pinkish-white eggs are laid. The white-shouldered black tit was formerly one of the many species in the genus Parus but was moved to Melaniparus after a molecular phylogenetic analysis published in 2013 showed that birds in the new genus formed a distinct clade. References Harrap and Quinn, Tits, Nuthatches and Treecreepers ISBN 0-7136-3964-4

nodes.summary: White-shouldered black tit（Melaniparus guineensis）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联1种鸟类，如White-shouldered black tit。

nodes.name: Melaniparus
nodes.summary: Melaniparus（Melaniparus）是图谱中的分类级分类节点，当前关联14种鸟类，如White-winged black tit、Rufous-bellied tit、White-bellied tit等14种鸟类。
